arts.ttl: Pires starts: Burnley team news and predicted XI to face Derby County
arts.sum: The latest Burnley team news as Scott Parker's side gear up to face Derby County in the Championship on Tuesday night. The post Pires starts: Burnley team news and predicted XI to face Derby County appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Paul Warne clarifies Derby County 'sugar' comments as he explains Leeds United strategy
arts.sum: Paul Warne does not regret his post-match comments after Saturday's defeat to Leeds United and said he has explained what he meant to the dressing room. In an interview with BBC Derby after the game, Warne said Derby were a "League One team with some sugar on top" against what he deemed a Premier League side as Daniel Farke's side recorded a 2-0 victory.

arts.ttl: Burnley FC manager Scott Parker does not expect one side to run away with league
arts.sum: Burnley boss Scott Parker expects the rest of the Championship season to be “nip and tuck” after his side and table toppers Sheffield United both dropped points over the weekend.

arts.ttl: Derby County injury news: Two back but two out for Rams
arts.sum: Derby picked up no fresh injuries from their defeat to Elland Road but will be without a couple of players whose absences have been acutely felt. No timescale has been put on his return and he will be out of tomorrow night's trip to Burnley.

arts.ttl: Derby County transfer news latest as enquiries made and rivals emerge for rookie goal machine
arts.sum: Paul Warne says he has had a couple of "casual" enquiries for players who could leave Derby County in next month's transfer window. Warne said he has told the players for whom he has received enquiries to make them aware of their options ahead of what is a pivotal month.

arts.ttl: Former Derby County coach leaves Championship rivals as Wayne Rooney explains split
arts.sum: Former Derby County coach Pete Shuttleworth has left their Championship rivals Plymouth Argyle. Shuttleworth has left Plymouth via mutual consent, a decision that boss Wayne Rooney said was due to "personal matters".
